synced:
[00:17.37] Just when I thought I got him to fall in love with Tennessee
[00:25.85] I shoulda known better than to take him back to Abilene
[00:34.21] I put him right back into her arms
[00:38.85] I wasn't a match for that kinda spark

[00:42.57] She's from Texas I can tell by the way
[00:46.65] He's two steppin' round the room
[00:51.01] And judgin' by the smile that's written on his face
[00:55.53] There's nothin' I can to do
[00:59.93] It doesn't take a crystal ball to see
[01:04.06] A cowboy always finds a way to leave
[01:08.21] Drinkin' Jack all by myself
[01:12.21] He's choosin' Texas I can tell

[01:23.65] Well, I guess he forgot about the smoky mountain rain
[01:32.05] Them old Hank tunes the Memphis blues we used sing
[01:40.81] He always loved Amarillo By Morning
[01:45.22] I shoulda taken that as warning

[01:48.93] She's from Texas I can tell by the way
[01:53.10] He's two steppin' round the room
[01:57.34] And judgin' by the smile that's written on his face
[02:02.09] There's nothin' I can to do
[02:06.33] It doesn't take a crystal ball to see
[02:10.78] A cowboy always finds a way to leave
[02:14.69] Drinkin' Jack all by myself
[02:19.01] He's choosin' Texas I can tell

[02:24.05] When I'm eastbound and down and I can't help but cry
[02:28.62] Cause I-40 gets lonelier with every mile
[02:32.85] I'll know that his mind wasn't ever gonna change
[02:37.14] Cause his heart still belongs to the lone star state

[02:44.66] She's from Texas I can tell by the way
[02:49.01] He's two steppin' round the room
[02:53.22] And judgin' by the smile that's written on his face
[02:57.90] There's nothin' I can to do, naw yeah
[03:02.35] It doesn't take a crystal ball to see
[03:06.46] A cowboy always finds a way to leave
[03:10.42] Drinkin' Jack all by myself
[03:14.30] He's choosing Texas I can tell, no
[03:18.98] Drinkin' Jack all by myself
[03:22.99] He's choosin' Texas I can tell

[03:27.55] C'mon baby
[03:32.94] Ohh yeah
[03:36.62] Just when I thought I got him to fall in love with Tennessee
[03:44.10]

Drinkin' Jack all by myself
He's choosin' Texas I can tell

He always loved Amarillo By Morning
I shoulda taken that as warning

And judgin' by the smile that's written on his face
There's nothin' I can to do

A cowboy always finds a way to leave
Drinkin' Jack all by myself
He's choosin' Texas I can tell

When I'm eastbound and down and I can't help but cry
Cause I-40 gets lonelier with every mile
I'll know that his mind wasn't ever gonna change
Cause his heart still belongs to the lone star state

Drinkin' Jack all by myself
He's choosing Texas I can tell, no

Ohh yeah
